Chapter 2  Using Menus and Commands

Source: https://www.pcjs.org/documents/books/mspl13/basic/qblearn/

## Overview

This chapter teaches how to start QuickBASIC and use the Easy Menus.
Covers opening menus, using dialog boxes, on-line help, function keys,
and shortcut-key combinations.

## Starting QuickBASIC

    Type qb at the DOS prompt.

Press ENTER at the welcome screen, then ESC to clear it.

## The QuickBASIC Environment

### The Menu Bar

    File  Edit  View  Search  Run  Debug  Options              Help

Menus contain QuickBASIC environment commands used while writing and
modifying programs in the View window.

### The Reference Bar

Located at the bottom of the screen. Displays:
- Shortcut key buttons for frequently used commands
- Brief descriptions when commands are highlighted
- Row and column counters
- CAPS LOCK and NUM LOCK indicators

### Opening Menus

- Press ALT to activate the menu bar
- Press the highlighted letter of the menu name
- Or press ALT+highlighted letter directly
- Use DIRECTION keys to move between menus and commands
- Press ENTER to choose the highlighted command
- Press ESC to close a menu without choosing a command

## QuickBASIC's On-Line Help

- Press F1 for context-sensitive help on the keyword at the cursor
- Press SHIFT+F1 for general help information
- Each help screen has hyperlinks to related topics

## Choosing Commands

Menus contain commands that perform actions in the environment.
Three dots (...) after a command name indicate a dialog box will appear.

## Using Dialog Boxes

### Anatomy of a Dialog Box

- **Text boxes** — Where you type information (e.g., file names)
- **List boxes** — Scrollable lists of items (e.g., files, directories)
- **Option buttons** — Mutually exclusive choices: ( ) or (*)
- **Check boxes** — Toggle options on/off: [ ] or [X]
- **Command buttons** — Actions: < OK >, < Cancel >, < Help >

### Navigation

- TAB moves between elements
- DIRECTION keys move within lists and option groups
- ENTER selects the current command button or confirms
- ESC cancels the dialog box

## Issuing Commands Directly

### Shortcut Keys for Commands

| Key         | Action                                |
|-------------|---------------------------------------|
| F5          | Run/Continue program                  |
| F1          | Context-sensitive help                |
| SHIFT+F1    | General help                          |
| ALT+F, X    | Exit QuickBASIC                       |

## Exiting from QuickBASIC

Press ALT+F, then X to choose File menu's Exit command.
QuickBASIC prompts to save unsaved changes.
Display settings saved in QB.INI file on exit.
